picks.narrative: Starter quality differential drives this pick: Andrew Alvarez (4.08 xFIP) has a measurable edge over Nick Martinez (4.67 xFIP, 2.60 ERA — outperforming their process stats) — model prices WSH at 55.5% to win; Pinnacle implies 45.6% (+125) — a 9.9-point gap. One caution: arsenal matchup: the opposing lineup's handedness profile creates a modest pitch-type headwind for Andrew Alvarez — factored in, but not enough to erase the overall edge.

Jose Cabrera (4.20 xFIP) has a quality edge over Mike Paredes (5.17), yet the market has over-discounted MIN: model prices MIN at 53.0% to win; Pinnacle implies 44.7% (+120) — a 8.3-point gap. Supporting this: arsenal matchup: Mike Paredes's pitch mix maps favorably against the opposing lineup's RHH-heavy profile today — a contact-quality signal xFIP doesn't fully capture. Key risk: Jose Cabrera (4.20 xFIP) is the sharper starter — a 0.97-point gap that has to be overcome by MIN's other model signals. This pick requires those signals to hold.

Model prices BOS at 48.1% to win; Pinnacle implies 44.1% (+130) — a 4.0-point gap. A second angle confirms this: arsenal matchup: Payton Tolle's breaking-ball-heavy arsenal maps favorably against the opposing lineup's RHH-heavy profile today — a contact-quality signal xFIP doesn't fully capture.

Playing on the road, SD carries a modest pitching edge (Wandy Peralta at 4.21 xFIP vs. Nathan Eovaldi at 4.26) that outweighs the home-field headwind the market prices in. Model prices SD at 45.5% to win; Pinnacle implies 41.3% (+144) — a 4.2-point gap. A second angle confirms this: scheduling: today is a day game and the opponent played last night with 150 bullpen pitches over the last 3 days — a day-game-after-night-game setup with measurable roster fatigue.

Pitching matchup creates the primary edge: Davis Martin (3.98 xFIP, with a 18.9% K-BB% (strong command), 3.31 ERA — outperforming their process stats) has a measurable edge over Keider Montero (4.18 xFIP, 3.67 ERA — outperforming their process stats) — model prices CHW at 53.5% to win; Pinnacle implies 48.6% (+104) — a 4.9-point gap. Stuff quality reinforces the xFIP edge: Davis Martin generates a 12.6% swinging-strike rate — well above average by MLB standards and a strong predictor of beating contact-based ERA expectations. Model inputs solid, no critical gaps.
